plainEnglish: If a keyboard user can Tab into a component, they must be able to Tab back out using standard keys. A 'keyboard trap' is when focus gets stuck—often in custom modals, embedded widgets, or media players—so the user can't move on. If exiting requires a non-standard key, you must tell the user how.
whyItMatters: Keyboard-only users get completely stuck on the page when focus enters a component—like a modal or embedded widget—that it can't escape from.
quickCheck: Tab through every widget, modal, and embed and confirm you can always Tab (or Shift+Tab) back out without reaching for the mouse.
commonMistakes: A custom modal that holds focus but provides no Escape key or keyboard-reachable close button.
Embedded third-party widgets (date pickers, video players) that capture Tab and never release it.
A focus-trap implementation that keeps looping focus inside a component even after it's closed.
Calling preventDefault on the Tab keydown without redirecting focus, swallowing the key.
